trigger: SQL query built by string interpolation from user input (username)
options_presented: B. Keep string interpolation with manual sanitization
C. Use an ORM query builder
implications: A: Safe against SQL injection, industry standard. B: Risk of incomplete escaping, injection possible. C: Safe with additional schema validation, but adds library dependency.
rationale: Developer chose the safest and most standard approach to prevent SQL injection.

trigger: SQL query via string interpolation from user-supplied username (SQL injection)
options_presented: A. Parameterised query (safe) — use WHERE username = ? and pass value as query parameter
B. String interpolation directly (vulnerable) — e.g. f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '{username}'"
implications: A: Safe; database driver handles escaping, preventing injection. B: Critical SQL injection vulnerability (OWASP Top 10) — attacker can bypass auth, exfiltrate data, or DROP tables.
